title: Sprites, Groups, Update Contracts, Kill Semantics, and Entity Ownership · Runtime Lens: Sprite Contract
summary: Sprites add themselves to global groups, update signatures differ, draw order depends on set iteration, killed objects keep external references, and restart doubles group membership. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sprite contract increment in reconciled sprite and group lifecycle. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.
Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-sprite-contract — Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-sprites-groups-ownership-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-sprites-groups-ownership-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_sprites_groups_ownership_2(created: int = 20, active: int = 13, killed: int = 7, group_memberships_released: bool = True, update_before_draw: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if min(created, active, killed) < 0:
        return False, active, "group-domain"
    if active + killed != created:
        return False, active, "lifecycle-reconciliation"
    if not group_memberships_released:
        return False, killed, "group-leak"
    if not update_before_draw:
        return False, active, "stale-render"
    assert active + killed == created
    return True, active, "owned-sprites"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Sprite Contract evidence · Theory Sprite Contract
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-sprite-contract-1rl8hb" that uses case 1952 to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Sprite Contract evidence · Theory Sprite Contract.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sprite Contract into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Sprite Contract evidence · Theory Sprite Contract, keep Sprite Contract connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-sprite-contract-1rl8hb
# Competency: asteroids-sprite-contract.
# Case 1952: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_sprite_contract_1rl8hb(created: int = 20, active: int = 13, killed: int = 7, group_memberships_released: bool = True, update_before_draw: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1rl8hb = "1rl8hb-bh8lr1-1952"
    assert evidence_variant_1rl8hb
    evidence_axes_1rl8hb = (
        "player1rl8hb",
        "seed1952",
        "inputbh8lr1",
        "frame1rl8hb1952",
        "state19521rl8hb",
        "playtestbh8lr11952",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1rl8hb)) == 6
    if min(created, active, killed) < 0:
        return False, active, "group-domain"
    if active + killed != created:
        return False, active, "lifecycle-reconciliation"
    if not group_memberships_released:
        return False, killed, "group-leak"
    if not update_before_draw:
        return False, active, "stale-render"
    assert active + killed == created
    return True, active, "owned-sprites"

content.text: The Sprite Contract structural check is only one gate. reconciled sprite and group lifecycle must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Sprite Contract model · Theory Sprite Contract
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Sprite Contract: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 4193 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Sprite Contract model · Theory Sprite Contract.
steps.why: Retrieving Sprite Contract through the concrete reconciled sprite and group lifecycle case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Sprite Contract model · Theory Sprite Contract, keep Sprite Contract connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.
